prequiz.title: MSSQL Query Concepts Keywords
prequiz.subtitle: Key terms for understanding SQL Server queries.
prequiz.description: Review these core concepts before starting the quiz. Some impostor terms (Pokémon) are mixed in for fun!

keywords.definition: Statement used to retrieve data from tables.
Combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column.
Clause to filter rows in a query.
Clause to aggregate rows based on column values.
Clause to filter groups after aggregation.
Clause to sort the result set.
Database structure that improves query performance.
Common Table Expression, a temporary result set for organizing queries.
Limits the number of rows returned.
Represents missing or unknown data.
A sequence of operations performed as a single logical unit.
Controls visibility of changes between transactions.
A situation where two or more transactions block each other.
A query nested inside another query.
Temporary name for a table or column in a query.
Pokémon impostor! Not a SQL concept.
Removes duplicate rows from the result set.
SQL Server's strategy for running a query.
Using parameters in queries to improve security and performance.
Pokémon impostor! Not a SQL concept.
